narrative: You notice a low growing plant with feathery leaves pushing up through the gravel at the edge of the path. You crush a flower head between your fingers...
interesting_fact: Pineapple Weed can flower just 37 days after germination — one of the fastest flowering plants in Britain. It became one of the most rapidly spreading plants of the 20th century after escaping from Kew Gardens.

questions.question: Which of these plants is Pineapple Weed?
Does Pineapple Weed have any other common names?
options: Wild Chamomile
Goosegrass
Winterweed
Alehoof

questions.question: What is the botanical name of Pineapple Weed?
options: Matricaria discoidea
Matricaria chamomilla
Anthemis cotula
Tanacetum vulgare

questions.question: Where is Pineapple Weed typically found?
options: Paths, gateways, compacted and disturbed ground
Deep shaded woodland floors near tree roots
Boggy wetland margins and riverbanks
Rocky coastal cliffs and exposed hillsides
questions.correct: Paths, gateways, compacted and disturbed ground

questions.question: Select all key identifying features of Pineapple Weed
options: Finely divided feathery leaves
Strong pineapple scent when crushed
Small dome shaped yellow-green flower heads with no petals
Cone shaped green buds before flowering
Tiny hooked hairs that cling to clothing
Heart shaped leaves in groups of three
correct: Small dome shaped yellow-green flower heads with no petals
Cone shaped green buds before flowering

questions.question: What is Pineapple Weed used for? Select all that apply
options: Flower heads eaten raw as a trail nibble
Made into a tea or syrup
Used as an insect repellent
Stir fried like tenderstem broccoli
Seeds dried as a coffee substitute
correct: Flower heads eaten raw as a trail nibble
Made into a tea or syrup

questions.question: What does Pineapple Weed taste like?
options: Sweet and fruity — like pineapple crossed with chamomile
Bitter and earthy with a slightly medicinal aftertaste
Sharp and lemony due to oxalic acid
Mild and vegetal — fresh and green with a slight bitterness
questions.correct: Sweet and fruity — like pineapple crossed with chamomile

questions.question: What are the cautions with Pineapple Weed?
options: Some people are allergic — try a small amount first. Avoid during pregnancy as it has historically been used for uterine conditions
Safe in small quantities but avoid entirely if you have liver or kidney conditions
Avoid in large quantities as it contains oxalic acid which can cause digestive issues
Generally safe but avoid if pregnant as it contains compounds similar to mint
questions.correct: Some people are allergic — try a small amount first. Avoid during pregnancy as it has historically been used for uterine conditions

questions.question: Which species could Pineapple Weed be confused with?
options: Mayweed Chamomile (Anthemis cotula) — similar but no pineapple smell and grows taller
Hemlock (Conium maculatum) — similar feathery leaves but grows much taller and is deadly
Yarrow (Achillea millefolium) — similar feathery leaves but has white flowers in flat clusters
Wild Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) — similar flowers but has white ray petals
questions.correct: Mayweed Chamomile (Anthemis cotula) — similar but no pineapple smell and grows taller

questions.question: When does Pineapple Weed flower?
options: May to November
December to February
July to August only
March to April only

questions.question: What time of year is Pineapple Weed most commonly found?
options: Late spring to autumn
Winter only when other plants have died back
Midsummer only during the warmest months
Year round including through winter

questions.question: Is Pineapple Weed native to the UK?
options: No — introduced from Asia, believed to have escaped from Kew Gardens in the 18th century
Yes — it is fully native and has grown wild in Britain since before Roman times
questions.correct: No — introduced from Asia, believed to have escaped from Kew Gardens in the 18th century

questions.question: What was Pineapple Weed historically used to treat? Select all that apply
options: Digestive complaints
Fevers
Menstrual pain
Broken bones

questions.question: How was Pineapple Weed applied medicinally? Select all that apply
options: As a tea or infusion
Rubbed on skin as an insect repellent
Burned and inhaled as smoke
Ground into a powder and swallowed dry
correct: Rubbed on skin as an insect repellent

questions.question: What is the life cycle of Pineapple Weed?
options: Annual — completes in one year
Perennial — returns every year from the same root
Biennial — takes two years to flower and set seed
Ephemeral — only lasts a few weeks before dying back

questions.question: What plant family does Pineapple Weed belong to?
options: Asteraceae — the daisy family
Lamiaceae — the mint family
Rosaceae — the rose family
Plantaginaceae — the plantain family

questions.question: What is the origin of the name Matricaria?
options: From Latin matrix meaning womb — due to its association with uterine treatments
Named after the botanist Maria Matricara who first classified it
From Greek meaning small flower — referring to its tiny flower heads
From Celtic meaning path plant — due to where it grows
questions.correct: From Latin matrix meaning womb — due to its association with uterine treatments

questions.question: What does Discoidea mean in the botanical name?
options: Without rays — referring to its lack of petals
Disc shaped — referring to the flat rosette of leaves
Small disc — referring to the miniature size of the plant
Double flower — referring to the two types of florets it produces
questions.correct: Without rays — referring to its lack of petals
